introduction:
In Rajasthan, the Jati Praman Patra (Caste Certificate) is issued by the Tehsildar / SDM (Sub-Divisional Magistrate) under the Revenue Department for SC, ST, OBC, and SBC (Special Backward Class) applicants.

Citizens can apply online through the Rajasthan SSO Portal (sso.rajasthan.gov.in) via the e-Mitra service or offline at the Tehsil / e-Mitra kiosk. Rajasthan also has a special SBC (Special Backward Class) category in addition to OBC.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by Tehsildar / SDM of the applicant's native tehsil
  • Processing time: 7–15 working days
  • Fee: ₹20–₹40 (via e-Mitra)
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC/SBC certificates valid for 3 years
  • Online via: Rajasthan SSO Portal → e-Mitra
  • Rajasthan has a distinct SBC (Special Backward Class) category
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Driving Licence
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • Class X / School Certificate showing caste
  • Land Records / Jamabandi of native village
  • Old Ration Card with caste
Additional (OBC/SBC)
  • Income Certificate (Non-Creamy Layer)
  • Domicile Certificate (Rajasthan)
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
  • Self-declaration affidavit
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Rajasthan SSO / e-Mitra Portal

Apply at Rajasthan SSO Portal

  1. Visit SSO Portal
    Go to sso.rajasthan.gov.in and login or register.
  2. Open e-Mitra
    After login, click on e-Mitra from the dashboard.
  3. Select Service
    Search for 'Jati Praman Patra' or 'Caste Certificate' and select the appropriate service.
  4. Fill Application
    Enter applicant name, DOB, caste category, tehsil, district, and other required details.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of all required documents.
  6. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹20–₹40 via online payment (UPI / Debit Card / Net Banking).
  7. Submit & Track
    Submit the application and track using your receipt / application number.
  8. Download Certificate
    Download the digitally signed caste certificate once approved by the Tehsildar.
You can also apply at any e-Mitra kiosk near your home.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / SDM Office

  1. Visit Tehsil or SDM Office
    Visit the Tehsildar / SDM office of your native tehsil in Rajasthan.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Jati Praman Patra form and fill all required details.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit the form with self-attested document copies and pay the fee at the counter.
  4. Patwari Verification
    The Patwari (Village Revenue Officer) will verify caste details and residential records.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect the caste certificate from the Tehsildar office within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at Rajasthan SSO / e-Mitra Portal using your application/receipt number.

OBC/SBC Renewal

OBC/SBC certificates are valid for 3 years. Renew via e-Mitra before expiry.

Grievance

Call 181 (Rajasthan Sampark Helpline) or raise complaint at Rajasthan Sampark Portal.
